headline: Lucid Group Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) executives emphasized an expanded autonomy partnership, new financing, and a renewed focus on cost and execution during the company’s first-quarter 2026 earnings call, while also suspending prior guidance as incoming CEO Silvio Napoli begins a review of the business. headline: What Block (SQ)'s New AI Commerce Tools and Partnerships Mean For Shareholders
summary: In late April 2026, Bank of America Securities began covering Block, highlighting AI-driven efficiency efforts alongside new products such as Square’s Managerbot and expanded partnerships with merchants and platforms like Uber Eats and Black Seed Bagels. These developments underline how Block is using artificial intelligence and integrated commerce tools to deepen its role in both consumer payments and complex multi-location business operations. headline: Uber (NYSE:UBER) Misses Q1 CY2026 Revenue Estimates, But Stock Soars 8.3%
summary: Ride sharing and on-demand delivery platform Uber (NYSE:UBER) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 14.5% year on year to $13.2 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.72 per share was 3.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Uber (NYSE:UBER) Misses Q1 CY2026 Revenue Estimates, But Stock Soars 8.3%
content.summary: Ride sharing and on-demand delivery platform Uber (NYSE:UBER) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 14.5% year on year to $13.2 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.72 per share was 3.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Uber forecasts strong second-quarter bookings despite Middle East woes
summary: Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday on strong demand for ride-hailing and delivery services, even though the Middle East ‌conflict weighed on its growth.  Uber said it expects gross bookings of $56.25 billion to $57.75 billion for the June ‌quarter, above analysts' average estimate of $56.07 ⁠billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.  It factors in a roughly 60 basis-point drag from the Middle East conflict.

content.title: Uber forecasts strong second-quarter bookings despite Middle East woes
content.summary: Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday on strong demand for ride-hailing and delivery services, even though the Middle East ‌conflict weighed on its growth.  Uber said it expects gross bookings of $56.25 billion to $57.75 billion for the June ‌quarter, above analysts' average estimate of $56.07 ⁠billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.  It factors in a roughly 60 basis-point drag from the Middle East conflict.

headline: Volkswagen And Partners Back Rivian Funding As Growth And Dilution Weigh
summary: Volkswagen increases its stake in Rivian Automotive (NasdaqGS:RIVN) through a large private share placement under an amended investment agreement. Rivian completes an additional capital raise with SMB Holding and Uber, adding new institutional backing. These equity investments are tied to Rivian’s future product and technology roadmap and deepen its partnerships with major industry players. Rivian focuses on electric trucks, SUVs, and commercial delivery vehicles, a segment that continues...
